demographics: I am 37 years old. I am female. I am Black or African American. I have never been married. I completed high school (12th grade). My family income is $30,000 to $34,999. I am a strong Democrat. I am slightly liberal. My religion is Catholic. I attend religious services about once a month.

events.event_text: Last year, when I was 36, I became actively involved in the local Black Lives Matter chapter. I started attending weekly meetings and participating in peaceful protests, driven by my personal experiences and a desire for change.

When I was 25, I was wrongfully terminated from my job at a local bakery. My manager accused me of something I didn't do, and despite my protests, I was let go without warning. This experience left me feeling betrayed and struggling financially for months. It also made me more determined to fight for fair treatment in the workplace.

When I was 28, my brother was arrested for a crime he didn't commit. I spent months trying to navigate the legal system, seeking help from various organizations, including our parish's community support network. My faith was tested, but the support of our religious community kept me going through that difficult time.

When I was 22, I got a job at a new factory that offered better pay and benefits. However, once hired, I realized it was a front for modern-day slavery, with excessive hours, low pay, and threats for anyone who complained. I knew I had to leave for my own safety, but losing this job meant I'd have to start over again.

At 22, I discovered that my parish priest had been involved in a financial scandal, using church funds for personal expenses. This shook my faith and trust in the institution, but also led me to become more involved in advocating for transparency and accountability within our congregation.

At 25, I was denied a promotion despite being the most qualified, realizing the racial bias in my company's HR practices after discovering they varied in pay and opportunity between black and white employees.

At 32, I was confronted with racial profiling when stopped by police while visiting my brother's family in another neighborhood. This event was a stark reminder of the struggles faced by many in our community and redoubled my commitment to activism.

compressed_summaries.summary: This person, at 36, became an active member of the Black Lives Matter chapter, attending weekly meetings and participating in protests, driven by personal experiences and a desire for change. At 25, they were wrongfully terminated from a job, leading to financial struggles and a determination to fight for fair workplace treatment. At 28, their brother was arrested for a crime he didn't commit, testing their faith but also strengthening their religious community's support.

This person, at 22, took a job at a factory promising better pay and benefits, but discovered it was a front for modern-day slavery, leading to a difficult decision to leave for safety despite the setback. At 22, they also found their parish priest involved in a financial scandal, which shook their faith but motivated them to advocate for transparency within their congregation. At 25, they were denied a promotion despite being the most qualified, realizing racial bias in their company's HR practices.

persona_card: This person is a Catholic who attends services monthly, balancing their traditional faith with a critical eye towards institutional transparency, shaped by past experiences with corruption. They hold strong pro-redistribution economic views, advocating for government intervention to address class disparities, while also being highly conscious of their working-class status. This individual subscribes to structural explanations for racial inequality, driven by personal experiences with discrimination and activism in Black Lives Matter. They have low trust in institutions, influenced by past encounters with injustice and wrongdoing, yet maintain openness towards social outgroups, including immigrants, racial minorities, and LGBTQ individuals, fostering a welcoming and inclusive atmosphere.
